title: They Billed $30M for Fake Kids' Therapy. The FBI Found Bentleys.
script: A $30 million Medicaid fraud ring was dismantled in Ohio this week. The suspects — including 2 Ohio state employees — allegedly billed for fake mental health therapy at children's summer camps and church groups that never happened. When federal agents moved in, they seized 14 luxury vehicles: Bentleys, McLarens, Maseratis, and 6 Mercedes. The FBI director flew to Ohio personally to announce the charges. The children never got therapy.
